Below is a **complete structured knowledge base** you can directly use for your **Expert System**. I organized everything exactly how a **trainer decision system** would work:

* Goal
* Experience level (Beginner → Intermediate → Advanced)
* Weekly workout split
* Exercises per day

This also follows common **exercise science guidelines** where beginners train **2–3 days/week**, intermediate **3–4 days/week**, and advanced trainees **4–5+ days/week** with increasing intensity and volume. ([Body Muscle Matters][1])

---

# 1️⃣ FAT LOSS WORKOUT PLAN

Fat loss training focuses on **strength + cardio** to increase calorie expenditure and maintain muscle mass.

---

# FAT LOSS — Beginner (3 Days)

### Day 1 – Upper Body

Chest, Shoulders, Arms

Exercises

* Dumbbell Bench Press
* Lat Pulldown Machine
* Shoulder Press Machine
* Dumbbell Bicep Curl
* Cable Tricep Pushdown

Cardio

* 15–20 min treadmill

---

### Day 2 – Lower Body + Core

Exercises

* Leg Press Machine
* Walking Lunges
* Leg Curl Machine
* Standing Calf Raises
* Plank

Cardio

* 15 min incline walk

---

### Day 3 – Cardio + Abs

Cardio

* Treadmill running – 20 min
* Stationary bike – 15 min

Core

* Russian Twists
* Leg Raises
* Mountain Climbers

---

# FAT LOSS — Intermediate (4 Days)

### Day 1 – Upper Body Push

Exercises

* Barbell Bench Press
* Incline Dumbbell Press
* Shoulder Press
* Lateral Raises
* Tricep Dips

Cardio

* 15 min HIIT treadmill

---

### Day 2 – Lower Body

Exercises

* Barbell Squats
* Leg Press
* Romanian Deadlift
* Leg Curl Machine
* Calf Raises

Cardio

* Stair climber – 10 min

---

### Day 3 – Upper Body Pull

Exercises

* Lat Pulldown
* Seated Cable Row
* Dumbbell Rows
* Barbell Curl
* Hammer Curl

Cardio

* Rowing machine – 15 min

---

### Day 4 – Full Body Fat Burn

Exercises

* Kettlebell Swings
* Goblet Squats
* Push Ups
* Battle Ropes
* Jump Rope

Cardio

* 20 min cycling

---

# FAT LOSS — Advanced (5 Days)

### Day 1 – Chest + Cardio

* Bench Press
* Incline Dumbbell Press
* Cable Fly
* Push Ups
* 20 min HIIT treadmill

---

### Day 2 – Back + Cardio

* Deadlift
* Pull Ups
* Lat Pulldown
* Seated Row
* 15 min rowing

---

### Day 3 – Legs

* Barbell Squats
* Leg Press
* Walking Lunges
* Leg Curl
* Calf Raises

---

### Day 4 – Shoulders + Abs

* Shoulder Press
* Lateral Raise
* Rear Delt Fly
* Hanging Leg Raise
* Russian Twists

---

### Day 5 – HIIT + Conditioning

* Sprint intervals
* Kettlebell swings
* Box jumps
* Battle ropes
* Jump rope

---

# 2️⃣ MUSCLE GAIN WORKOUT PLAN

Muscle gain focuses on **progressive overload and hypertrophy training**.

---

# MUSCLE GAIN — Beginner (4 Days)

### Day 1 – Chest + Triceps

* Bench Press
* Incline Dumbbell Press
* Chest Fly Machine
* Tricep Pushdown
* Overhead Tricep Extension

---

### Day 2 – Back + Biceps

* Lat Pulldown
* Seated Row
* Dumbbell Row
* Barbell Curl
* Hammer Curl

---

### Day 3 – Legs

* Squats
* Leg Press
* Leg Extension
* Leg Curl
* Calf Raises

---

### Day 4 – Shoulders + Abs

* Shoulder Press
* Lateral Raises
* Rear Delt Fly
* Face Pull
* Hanging Leg Raises

---

# MUSCLE GAIN — Intermediate (5 Days)

### Day 1 – Chest

* Bench Press
* Incline Bench Press
* Dumbbell Fly
* Cable Fly

---

### Day 2 – Back

* Deadlift
* Pull Ups
* Lat Pulldown
* Seated Row

---

### Day 3 – Legs

* Squats
* Leg Press
* Romanian Deadlift
* Leg Curl
* Calf Raises

---

### Day 4 – Shoulders

* Shoulder Press
* Lateral Raise
* Rear Delt Fly
* Face Pull

---

### Day 5 – Arms

* Barbell Curl
* Hammer Curl
* Skull Crushers
* Cable Pushdown

---

# MUSCLE GAIN — Advanced (6 Days)

### Day 1 – Chest

* Bench Press
* Incline Press
* Chest Fly
* Dips

---

### Day 2 – Back

* Deadlift
* Pull Ups
* Barbell Row
* Lat Pulldown

---

### Day 3 – Legs

* Squats
* Leg Press
* Lunges
* Leg Curl
* Calf Raises

---

### Day 4 – Shoulders

* Military Press
* Lateral Raises
* Rear Delt Fly

---

### Day 5 – Arms

* Barbell Curl
* Preacher Curl
* Skull Crushers
* Cable Pushdown

---

### Day 6 – Core + Conditioning

* Hanging Leg Raises
* Russian Twists
* Plank
* Farmer Walk

---

# 3️⃣ ENDURANCE WORKOUT PLAN

Endurance focuses on **cardio + muscular endurance circuits** with higher repetitions and shorter rest periods. ([Back Nine Physical Therapy][2])

---

# ENDURANCE — Beginner (3 Days)

### Day 1 – Cardio

* Treadmill jog – 20 min
* Cycling – 10 min

---

### Day 2 – Light Strength Circuit

* Bodyweight Squats
* Push Ups
* Dumbbell Rows
* Jumping Jacks
* Plank

---

### Day 3 – Cardio + Core

* Rowing machine – 15 min
* Russian Twists
* Leg Raises

---

# ENDURANCE — Intermediate (4 Days)

### Day 1 – Cardio

* Running – 25 min

---

### Day 2 – Strength Circuit

* Squats
* Push Ups
* Pull Ups
* Lunges
* Plank

---

### Day 3 – HIIT Cardio

* Sprint intervals
* Jump rope

---

### Day 4 – Core + Conditioning

* Mountain Climbers
* Russian Twists
* Bicycle Crunches

---

# ENDURANCE — Advanced (5 Days)

### Day 1 – Long Distance Cardio

* Running – 40 min

---

### Day 2 – Full Body Circuit

* Squats
* Push Ups
* Pull Ups
* Kettlebell Swings

---

### Day 3 – HIIT

* Sprint intervals
* Battle ropes
* Jump rope

---

### Day 4 – Strength Endurance

* Deadlift
* Bench Press
* Lunges
* Plank

---

### Day 5 – Conditioning

* Rowing machine
* Stair climber
* Core circuit

---

# Example Output for Your Expert System

Input

```
Goal: Muscle Gain
Experience: Intermediate
Training Days: 5
```

Output

```
Recommended Workout Split:

Day 1: Chest
Day 2: Back
Day 3: Legs
Day 4: Shoulders
Day 5: Arms
```

Explanation

```
Because the user selected muscle gain and intermediate experience,
the system recommends a split routine that increases training volume
per muscle group to stimulate hypertrophy.
```
